what_it_does: Positions a fixed left-edge vertical navigation rail host for DNA's navRail and provides a degrade-clean, styled fallback nav that hides once DNA's rail renders.
observations: The host (.surface-nav) only controls placement: fixed left edge, vertically centered, z-index 22, max-height with overflow:auto.
DNA's navRail mounts into .surface-nav-dna; the host does not style the rail tiles.
The fallback is hidden with the sibling selector .surface-nav-dna:not(:empty) + .surface-nav-fallback.
Fallback items are pill-shaped chips with hover, focus-visible, and a blurred translucent background.
A media query increases the left offset at viewports >=1024px.
All sizing, color, typography, and radius values come from CSS custom properties.
standouts: Progressive-enhancement swap: DNA rail preferred, plain fallback shown while DNA is empty.
Host/fallback separation: geometry lives here, DNA owns the rendered rail look.
Sibling :not(:empty) combinator used as the load-state gate.
conventions: Design-token-driven styling via CSS custom properties.
Progressive enhancement with a no-JS-failure fallback.
Responsive layout via min-width media query.
concerns: z-index 22 is a magic number whose layering context against other shell chrome is not defined here.
overflow:auto on the fixed rail could clip focus outlines or introduce an unexpected nested scroll.
Fallback nav items have no visible active/current state.
No ARIA semantics are present in this CSS-only file.
notes: This file is the shell-level nav slot: it places the rail on the left edge and supplies a styled fallback that disappears once DNA's navRail mounts.
questions: What values do --s-2, --s-4, --s-7, --t-xs, --ink-dim, --ground-accent, etc. resolve to?
What markup/classes does DNA's navRail inject into .surface-nav-dna?
How are fallback nav items populated and how is the active route indicated?
How does z-index 22 relate to the wheel, overlays, and other shell chrome?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it owns placement and the functional link/fallback, while DNA refines the rail's own look.
apparent_themes: Separation of host geometry from component styling
intention_signals: The file is trying to guarantee an always-working left-edge navigation surface: a DNA-powered rail when available, and an immediately visible plain chip nav until then, with the host only controlling where the rail sits.
novelty: The progressive swap uses :not(:empty) on the DNA mount point plus an adjacent-sibling selector to remove the fallback without JavaScript state.
contribution: Defines the host geometry for the surface nav rail and a degrade-clean fallback style, ensuring a left-edge nav is present before or without DNA and that both navs never appear together.
summary_for_embedding: CSS that positions a fixed left-edge vertical nav rail host and styles a labelled pill-chip fallback nav that hides when DNA's navRail renders.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a shell-level nav slot with a degrade-clean fallback so navigation is always available while DNA's navRail loads.
